title: Matrics urged to register for second chance programme by 16 February
description: NATIONAL NEWS - The MEC for Education in...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"NATIONAL NEWS - The MEC for Education in KwaZulu-Natal, Sipho Hlomuka, has encouraged learners who did not meet the requirements for the 2025 National Senior Certificate (NSC) examinations to take advantage of the department’s Second Chance Programme.","html":"NATIONAL NEWS - The MEC for Education in KwaZulu-Natal, Sipho Hlomuka, has encouraged learners who did not meet the requirements for the 2025 National Senior Certificate (NSC) examinations to take advantage of the department’s Second Chance Programme."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Hlomuka said the programme offers learners a valuable opportunity to rewrite selected subjects and improve their results during the May/June 2026 examination sitting."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Not achieving the desired outcome in the NSC examinations does not define a learner’s future. The Second Chance Programme is a clear demonstration of our commitment to ensuring that every learner is given an opportunity to succeed,” said Hlomuka.","html":"“Not achieving the desired outcome in the NSC examinations does not define a learner’s future. The Second Chance Programme is a clear demonstration of our commitment to ensuring that every learner is given an opportunity to succeed,” said Hlomuka."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The MEC announced that registrations for the programme will be open until Friday, 6 February 2026.","html":"The MEC announced that registrations for the programme will be open until Friday, 6 February 2026."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Eligible learners are encouraged to register at their nearest district office or designated centre where they will receive guidance on subject selection, registration requirements, and available academic support.","html":"Eligible learners are encouraged to register at their nearest district office or designated centre where they will receive guidance on subject selection, registration requirements, and available academic support."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The initiative will provide structured revision, curriculum-focused support, and access to learning materials to help learners address their weaknesses identified during the NSC examinations.","html":"The initiative will provide structured revision, curriculum-focused support, and access to learning materials to help learners address their weaknesses identified during the NSC examinations."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Hlomuka further called on parents, guardians, and communities to support learners during this process, stressing that collective encouragement plays a critical role in learner success.","html":"Hlomuka further called on parents, guardians, and communities to support learners during this process, stressing that collective encouragement plays a critical role in learner success."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“The Second Chance Programme is exactly that, a second opportunity to rise, refocus, and achieve. We urge our learners to seize it with determination and confidence,” he said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"KwaZulu-Natal achieved an impressive 90.6% matric pass rate.","html":"KwaZulu-Natal achieved an impressive 90.6% matric pass rate."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This stellar performance aligns with a national trend, as the country reached its highest-ever pass rate of 88%, marking a 0.7% increase from 2024.","html":"This stellar performance aligns with a national trend, as the country reached its highest-ever pass rate of 88%, marking a 0.7% increase from 2024."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Despite facing significant budget challenges in 2025, the MEC  said he remains optimistic about the 2026 academic year.","html":"Despite facing significant budget challenges in 2025, the MEC said he remains optimistic about the 2026 academic year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He told SAnews this week that the province has already begun the early opening of schools and is focusing on completing the syllabus ahead of time to allow for revision. – SAnews.gov.za","html":"He told SAnews this week that the province has already begun the early opening of schools and is focusing on completing the syllabus ahead of time to allow for revision. – SAnews.gov.za"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Matrics urged to register for second chance programme by 16 February
